req.title: Create encounter (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-031 mandates that a clinician can create an Encounter with valid start/end timestamps and that end-before-start is rejected with HTTP 422. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive — a well-formed encounter with end > start returns 201; (2) NEG negative — end < start returns 422; (3) BVA boundary — end == start (zero-duration) sits at the exact boundary and should be accepted (inclusive boundary); (4) DT negative — end exactly 1 second before start, confirming the rejection rule fires for the minimal violation. Together these cover the valid partition, the error partition, and both sides of the temporal boundary.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token> fixture
A valid patient resource exists; its ID is available via <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /Encounter with resourceType=Encounter, status=in-progress, start=2024-06-01T08:00:00Z, end=2024-06-01T09:00:00Z
expected: Response body contains an id field
trace: R-HLT-031: encounter stored when end > start
EP: payload belongs to the valid equivalence class

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token> fixture
A valid patient resource exists; its ID is available via <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /Encounter with start=2024-06-01T09:00:00Z, end=2024-06-01T08:00:00Z (end one hour before start)
expected: Response body contains a diagnostic or issue message referencing the invalid period
trace: NEG: payload belongs to the invalid-period equivalence class

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token> fixture
A valid patient resource exists; its ID is available via <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /Encounter with start == end == 2024-06-01T08:00:00Z (zero-duration, exact boundary)
expected: HTTP 201 Created (zero-duration encounter is at the inclusive boundary and must be accepted)
Response body contains an id field
trace: R-HLT-031: boundary case — end equals start is the minimum valid value
BVA: on-boundary value where end == start

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_one_second_before_start_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <auth_token> fixture
A valid patient resource exists; its ID is available via <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /Encounter with start=2024-06-01T08:00:01Z, end=2024-06-01T08:00:00Z (end exactly 1 second before start — off-boundary violation)
expected: Confirms the rule fires for the minimal temporal violation (1-second off-boundary)
trace: DT: decision rule — condition: end < start → action: reject with 422; minimal off-boundary case
